title: Logging with logger: namespaces and thresholds
text: logger defines seven levels least verbose: package default threshold INFO: DEBUG TRACE lines silent unless explicitly lower threshold.

title: Logging with logger: namespaces and thresholds
text: Every logger::log_*() call tooltipexplorer passes explicit namespace string. full set, registered app_set_log_threshold(), : "tooltipexplorer/<module>" convention means can silence one noisy module keeping others verbose — see examples .

title: Logging with logger: namespaces and thresholds
text: single entry point changing thresholds. called per session app_server() applies level every namespace table .
code: app_set_log_threshold <- function(level = logger::INFO) {   namespaces <- c(     "global",     "tooltipexplorer/app",     "tooltipexplorer/inputs",     "tooltipexplorer/outputs",     "tooltipexplorer/download",     "tooltipexplorer/tooltip",     "tooltipexplorer/hoverinfo"   )   for (ns in namespaces) {     logger::log_threshold(level, namespace = ns)   }   invisible(level) }

title: Logging with logger: namespaces and thresholds
text: can call app_set_log_threshold() interactively time app running — threshold change takes effect immediately subsequent log calls.
code: # Default (production) — INFO and above only tooltipexplorer::app_set_log_threshold(logger::INFO)  # Development — everything including DEBUG tooltipexplorer::app_set_log_threshold(logger::DEBUG)  # Silent except for warnings and errors tooltipexplorer::app_set_log_threshold(logger::WARN)

title: Logging with logger: namespaces and thresholds
text: calling app_set_log_threshold() can override individual namespaces logger::log_threshold() directly:
code: # Make the outputs module verbose while keeping everything else at INFO tooltipexplorer::app_set_log_threshold(logger::INFO) logger::log_threshold(logger::DEBUG, namespace = "tooltipexplorer/outputs")  # Silence the download module entirely logger::log_threshold(logger::FATAL, namespace = "tooltipexplorer/download")

title: Logging with logger: namespaces and thresholds
code: # Returns the current level for a given namespace logger::log_threshold(namespace = "tooltipexplorer/outputs")

title: Logging with logger: namespaces and thresholds
text: with_logging() tryCatch / withCallingHandlers wrapper used around expressions might produce warnings errors. logs , adds context label every message, re-issues condition Shiny caller continue see normally. with_logging() used three places app_server(): inside module server functions , wrapping individual output renderers:
code: with_logging <- function(expr, context = "", ns = "tooltipexplorer/app") {   tryCatch(     withCallingHandlers(       expr,       warning = function(w) {         logger::log_warn(           "[{context}] Warning: {conditionMessage(w)}",           namespace = ns         )         invokeRestart("muffleWarning")       }     ),     error = function(e) {       logger::log_error(         "[{context}] Error: {conditionMessage(e)}",         namespace = ns       )       stop(e)     }   ) } # Wiring the inputs module inputs_r <- with_logging(   mod_inputs_server("inputs"),   context = "app_server / mod_inputs_server",   ns      = "tooltipexplorer/app" )  # Wiring the outputs module perf_r <- with_logging(   mod_outputs_server("outputs", inputs_r = inputs_r),   context = "app_server / mod_outputs_server",   ns      = "tooltipexplorer/app" )  # Wiring the download module with_logging(   mod_download_server("download", inputs_r = inputs_r, perf_r = perf_r),   context = "app_server / mod_download_server",   ns      = "tooltipexplorer/app" ) output$bslib_boxes <- shiny::renderUI({   shiny::req(perf_r())   with_logging(     context = "mod_outputs / bslib_boxes",     ns      = "tooltipexplorer/outputs",     {       df <- perf_r()       logger::log_debug(         "Rendering bslib popover boxes | n: {nrow(df)}",         namespace = "tooltipexplorer/outputs"       )       # ... render code ...     }   ) })

title: Logging with logger: namespaces and thresholds
text: messages use logger’s glue-interpolation syntax — curly-brace expressions evaluated calling environment time log call made: key: value pattern (pipe-separated) makes easy grep specific field across log file:
code: # Structured fields separated by " | " logger::log_info(   "Fetching prices | tickers: [{paste(tickers, collapse = ', ')}] | from: {from} | to: {to}",   namespace = "tooltipexplorer/outputs" ) # → INFO [2026-04-03 08:00:00] Fetching prices | tickers: [AAPL, MSFT] | from: 2024-01-01 | to: 2024-12-31 grep "tickers:" app.log grep "ERROR" app.log grep "tooltipexplorer/outputs" app.log

title: Logging with logger: namespaces and thresholds
text: default logger writes console. add file appender alongside console appender: route specific namespace file:
code: # Append all INFO+ messages to a rotating log file logger::log_appender(   logger::appender_tee(file = "tooltipexplorer.log"),   namespace = "tooltipexplorer/app" ) logger::log_appender(   logger::appender_file("outputs.log"),   namespace = "tooltipexplorer/outputs" )

title: Logging with logger: namespaces and thresholds
code: # 1. Load the package devtools::load_all()  # 2. Make all namespaces verbose tooltipexplorer::app_set_log_threshold(logger::DEBUG)  # 3. (Optional) focus on one noisy namespace only logger::log_threshold(logger::DEBUG, namespace = "tooltipexplorer/outputs")  # 4. Launch — log messages stream to the console as you interact tooltipexplorer::launch()

title: Logging with logger: namespaces and thresholds
text: logger package documentation ?tooltipexplorer::app_set_log_threshold ?tooltipexplorer::with_logging

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
text: module lives single file containing _ui() _server() functions. exported objects one file .
code: R/ ├── app_server.R              # app_server() ├── app_set_log_threshold.R   # app_set_log_threshold() ├── app_ui.R                  # app_ui() ├── compute_rolling_vol.R     # compute_rolling_vol() ├── default_tickers.R         # default_tickers  (character vector) ├── get_ff3_factors.R         # get_ff3_factors() ├── get_stock_prices.R        # get_stock_prices() ├── get_stock_returns.R       # get_stock_returns() ├── launch.R                  # launch() ├── mod_download.R            # mod_download_ui()  +  mod_download_server() ├── mod_hoverinfo.R           # mod_hoverinfo() ├── mod_inputs.R              # mod_inputs_ui()    +  mod_inputs_server() ├── mod_outputs.R             # mod_outputs_ui()   +  mod_outputs_server() ├── mod_tooltip.R             # mod_tooltip() ├── summarise_performance.R   # summarise_performance() ├── utils_operators.R         # %||% └── with_logging.R            # with_logging()

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
text: launch() calls shiny::shinyApp(app_ui(), app_server) accepts ... forwarded shiny::shinyApp() (e.g. options = list(port = 4000)).
code: tooltipexplorer::launch()

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
text: download module UI (mod_download_ui("download")) embedded bottom inputs sidebar inside mod_inputs_ui(); server wired top level app_server().
code: launch()   └─ shinyApp(app_ui(), app_server)  app_ui()   ├─ mod_inputs_ui("inputs")       # sidebar: tickers, dates, vol window, fetch   └─ mod_outputs_ui("outputs")     # main: KPI boxes + 5 demo tabs  app_server()   ├─ mod_inputs_server("inputs")   → inputs_r  (reactive list)   ├─ mod_outputs_server("outputs", inputs_r)   │    ├─ shinyhelper::observe_helpers()   # registered here, per session   │    └─ returns perf_r  (reactive tibble)   └─ mod_download_server("download", inputs_r, perf_r)

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
text: modules follow mod_<name>_ui() / mod_<name>_server() convention co-located single mod_<name>.R file.

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
text: mod_inputs_ui(id) — returns bslib::sidebar() : selectizeInput — ticker picker (multi-select, user-creatable) dateRangeInput — date range sliderInput — rolling-volatility window (5–120 trading days) actionButton — “Fetch data” mod_download_ui() embedded bottom mod_inputs_server(id) — returns reactive list:
code: list(   tickers    = character(),   # selected ticker symbols   from       = Sys.Date(),    # start date   to         = Sys.Date(),    # end date   vol_window = 30L,           # rolling-vol window in days   fetch      = integer()      # action button counter )

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
text: mod_outputs_ui(id) — returns shiny::tagList() containing: shiny::uiOutput — KPI value boxes (one per ticker) bslib::navset_card_tab() — five demo tabs (see ) mod_outputs_server(id, inputs_r) — triggered inputs_r()$fetch: Calls shinyhelper::observe_helpers() per session (registered ) Calls get_stock_prices() → prices_r Calls get_stock_returns(prices_r()) → returns_r Calls summarise_performance(returns_r()) → perf_r Renders outputs (value boxes + five tabs) Returns perf_r download module

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
text: mod_download_ui(id) — bslib::card() format selector downloadButton. mod_download_server(id, inputs_r, perf_r) — renders parameterised inst/report_template.Rmd HTML PDF serves via shiny::downloadHandler().

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
text: Financial data fetched processed four functions:

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
code: library(tooltipexplorer)  prices  <- get_stock_prices(c("AAPL", "MSFT"), from = "2024-01-01") returns <- get_stock_returns(prices) perf    <- summarise_performance(returns) vol     <- compute_rolling_vol(returns, window = 30L)

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
text: Two helper functions provide uniform interface attaching tooltip hover content across five back-ends.

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
text: UI helper (mod_tooltip.R) — returns shiny.tag server-side counterpart. Place anywhere inside UI tree, including inside renderUI().
code: mod_tooltip(   trigger     = bsicons::bs_icon("info-circle"),  # default   type        = c("bslib", "shinyhelper", "prompter", "shinyalert"),   contents    = "",   size        = NULL,       # CSS font-size for the wrapper span   style       = NULL,       # extra inline CSS for the wrapper span   helper_type = "inline",   # "inline" | "markdown"  (shinyhelper only)   helper_size = "m",        # "s" | "m" | "l"        (shinyhelper only)   alert_type  = "info",     # "info"|"success"|"warning"|"error" (shinyalert only)   ...                       # forwarded to the back-end function )

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
code: mod_tooltip(   type     = "bslib",   contents = "Annualised log return = mean daily log return \u00d7 252.",   title    = "Ann. Return" )

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
code: mod_tooltip(   trigger     = shiny::tags$span("Sharpe Ratio"),   type        = "shinyhelper",   contents    = c("Sharpe Ratio", "Assumes a zero risk-free rate."),   helper_type = "inline",   helper_size = "m",   title       = "Sharpe Ratio" )

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
code: mod_tooltip(   trigger  = shiny::tags$span("Ann. Vol"),   type     = "prompter",   contents = "Annualised volatility = SD of daily log returns \u00d7 \u221a252.",   position = "right" )

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
text: shinyalert back-end stores content data-sa-* attributes fires click via delegated jQuery handler injected app_ui(). observeEvent() server handler needed.
code:
mod_tooltip( trigger = shiny::tags$span(class = "fs-4 fw-bold", "AAPL"), type = "shinyalert", contents = "Ann. Return: 14.2%
Ann. Vol: 22.1%
Sharpe: 0.64", title = "AAPL \u2013 Performance Summary", alert_type = "info", confirmButtonText = "Close" )

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
text: rendering helper (mod_hoverinfo.R) — formats hover content back-ends build tooltips programmatically inside table-cell renderers. Returns htmltools <span> title attribute — browsers render native tooltip hover. Use inside reactable::colDef(cell = ..., html = TRUE).
code: mod_hoverinfo(   type     = "reactable",   # currently the only supported back-end   contents = character(0),  # tooltip text; named vector → "Name: value" pairs   display  = NULL,          # visible cell value (child node of the <span>)   size     = NULL,          # CSS font-size   style    = NULL,          # inline CSS (e.g. "color:#198754; cursor:help")   ...                       # extra HTML attributes on the <span> ) reactable::colDef(   name = "Ann. Return (%)",   html = TRUE,   cell = function(value, index) {     mod_hoverinfo(       type     = "reactable",       contents = glue::glue("Annualised log return for {df$symbol[index]}: {value}%"),       display  = glue::glue("{value}%"),       style    = paste0(         "color:", if (value >= 0) "#198754" else "#dc3545",         "; cursor:help"       )     )   } )

title: Tooltip and Hover-Info Explorer
text: app_set_log_threshold() called app_server() session start. Pass logger::DEBUG development verbose output:
code: # In app_server(), or interactively: app_set_log_threshold(logger::DEBUG)

code: prices  <- get_stock_prices(c("AAPL", "MSFT"), from = "2024-01-01") returns <- get_stock_returns(prices) perf    <- summarise_performance(returns) vol     <- compute_rolling_vol(returns, window = 30L)  # Fama-French 3-factor data ff3 <- get_ff3_factors("2020-01-01", "2023-12-31")

text: mod_tooltip() provides single interface across click/hover back-ends: mod_hoverinfo() formats hover content reactable table cells:
code: # bslib popover (click) mod_tooltip(   type     = "bslib",   contents = "Annualised log return = mean daily log return \u00d7 252.",   title    = "Ann. Return" )  # prompter attribute tooltip (hover) mod_tooltip(   trigger  = shiny::tags$span("Ann. Vol"),   type     = "prompter",   contents = "Annualised volatility = SD of daily log returns \u00d7 \u221a252.",   position = "right" ) reactable::colDef(   name = "Ann. Return (%)",   html = TRUE,   cell = function(value, index) {     mod_hoverinfo(       type     = "reactable",       contents = glue::glue("Annualised log return for {df$symbol[index]}: {value}%"),       display  = glue::glue("{value}%"),       style    = paste0("color:", if (value >= 0) "#198754" else "#dc3545", "; cursor:help")     )   } )

title: Hover-info module — mod_hoverinfo
text: server-side helper formats hover/tooltip content reactable table cells.  Wraps display value htmltools <span> title attribute, browsers render native tooltip hover.

title: Hover-info module — mod_hoverinfo
text: type Back-end target.  Currently "reactable". contents character string (named character vector) used tooltip text title attribute.  Named vectors produce "Name: value" pairs joined " | ". display value render visibly inside cell.  Passed child node <span>.  Kept explicit parameter never accidentally matched size positional argument order. size CSS font-size span wrapper, e.g. "0.8rem". NULL (default) leaves unchanged. style Inline CSS span wrapper, e.g. "cursor:help". NULL (default) applies none. ... Additional HTML attributes passed <span> tag.

title: Hover-info module — mod_hoverinfo
text: htmltools <span> tag title attribute set. Use inside reactable::colDef(cell = ..., html = TRUE) renderer.

Return (%)",   html = TRUE,   cell = function(value, index) {     mod_hoverinfo(       type     = "reactable",       contents = paste0("Annualised log return: ", value, "%"),       display  = paste0(value, "%"),       style    = "color:#198754; cursor:help"     )   } ) } # }

title: Tooltip module — mod_tooltip
text: lightweight UI helper wraps several tooltip/popover/modal back-ends behind single, consistent interface.  Drop anywhere inside UI another module — paired server function required.

title: Tooltip module — mod_tooltip
code: mod_tooltip(   trigger = bsicons::bs_icon("info-circle"),   type = c("bslib", "shinyhelper", "prompter", "shinyalert"),   contents = "",   size = NULL,   style = NULL,   helper_type = c("inline", "markdown"),   helper_size = c("m", "s", "l"),   alert_type = c("info", "success", "warning", "error"),   ... )

title: Tooltip module — mod_tooltip
text:
trigger shiny.tag (plain text) tooltip attaches . Defaults small bsicons::bs_icon("info-circle"). type Back-end use. One "bslib" (default), "shinyhelper", "prompter", "shinyalert". contents Character string vector — tooltip/popover/modal body. HTML accepted "bslib". "shinyhelper" plain-text character vector preferred — elements joined
helper(type = "inline"), embedding HTML tags causes double-escaping. Ignored plain text "prompter". "shinyalert" HTML supported (rendered via html: true). size CSS font-size trigger wrapper span, e.g. "0.85rem". NULL (default) leaves font size unchanged. Note: purely CSS property wrapper — distinct helper_size (shinyhelper modal size). style Additional inline CSS trigger wrapper, e.g. "color:#6c757d". NULL (default) applies none. helper_type Content type forwarded shinyhelper::helper() type argument. One "inline" (default) "markdown". Ignored back-ends. helper_size Modal size forwarded shinyhelper::helper() size argument. One "s", "m" (default), "l". Ignored back-ends. Kept separate size avoid two params colliding. alert_type Alert style forwarded shinyalert() type argument. One "info" (default), "success", "warning", "error". Ignored back-ends. ... Extra arguments forwarded directly underlying back-end function: "bslib" → bslib::popover() (e.g. title, placement) "shinyhelper" → shinyhelper::helper() (e.g. title, colour, icon, buttonLabel, easyClose, fade) "prompter" → prompter::add_prompt() (e.g. position, rounded, bounce, arrow, animate) "shinyalert" → stored data-sa-* attributes; recognised keys title confirmButtonText

title: Tooltip module — mod_tooltip
text: shiny.tag (shiny.tagList) ready embed UI.

title: Tooltip module — mod_tooltip
text: mod_tooltip() type = "shinyalert"`` requires delegated jQuery click handler injected page. .e., inapp_ui.R`:
code: shiny::tags$script(shiny::HTML("   $(document).on('click', '[data-sa-title]', function() { ... }); "))

title: Tooltip module — mod_tooltip
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # bslib popover (click to open) mod_tooltip( type = "bslib", contents = "A bslib popover with HTML.", title = "More info" ) # shinyhelper inline help modal mod_tooltip( trigger = shiny::tags$span("Sharpe Ratio"), type = "shinyhelper", contents = c("Sharpe Ratio", "Assumes a zero risk-free rate."), helper_type = "inline", helper_size = "m", title = "Sharpe Ratio" ) # prompter attribute-driven tooltip mod_tooltip( trigger = bsicons::bs_icon("info-circle"), type = "prompter", contents = "Number of trading days in the rolling window.", position = "right" ) # shinyalert modal on click (requires sa-handler to be present in UI) mod_tooltip( trigger = shiny::tags$span("What is ann. vol?"), type = "shinyalert", contents = "Annualised volatility = SD of daily log returns x sqrt(252).", title = "Annualised Volatility", alert_type = "info" ) } # }

what: Summarise performance metrics by ticker — summarise_performance
title: Summarise performance metrics by ticker — summarise_performance
text: Computes annualised return, annualised volatility, Sharpe ratio (assuming zero risk-free rate) ticker.

title: Summarise performance metrics by ticker — summarise_performance
code: summarise_performance(returns)

title: Summarise performance metrics by ticker — summarise_performance
text: returns tibble returned get_stock_returns().

title: Summarise performance metrics by ticker — summarise_performance
text: tibble columns: symbol, ann_return, ann_vol, sharpe.

what: Execute an expression with structured error and warning logging — with_logging
title: Execute an expression with structured error and warning logging — with_logging
text: Wraps expr tryCatch() : logs warnings via logger::log_warn() re-issues Shiny can also handle logs errors via logger::log_error() re-throws caller / Shiny sees error normally

title: Execute an expression with structured error and warning logging — with_logging
code: with_logging(expr, context = "", ns = "tooltipexplorer/app")

title: Execute an expression with structured error and warning logging — with_logging
text: expr Expression evaluate (passed unevaluated via ...). context Short string identifying call site, e.g. "mod_outputs / prices_r".  Prepended every log message. ns Logger namespace string. Defaults "tooltipexplorer/app".

title: Execute an expression with structured error and warning logging — with_logging
text: value expr success; re-throws error.

title: Execute an expression with structured error and warning logging — with_logging
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- with_logging(   context = "my_module / compute",   ns      = "tooltipexplorer/app",   sqrt(4) ) } # }
